content: 2025 configurable ICM curiosity mechanisms can be structured to allocate intrinsic exploration reward dynamically based on the novelty of content stored in an agent's tiered short-term/long-term memory: novel observations that conflict with existing long-term memory schemas get a much larger curiosity reward than minor deviations only held in short-term memory. This creates a self-tuning curiosity drive that prioritizes high-impact exploratory learning over redundant, low-value novelty-seeking.
